generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically retrieve and refresh all Event and Activity data from both Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 on a fixed 60-second cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 mandates a 60-second automated refresh cycle as the core real-time situational awareness mechanism.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a Multi-Document Interface (MDI) layout in which the left two-thirds of the screen is permanently allocated to a graphical timeline view and the right one-third is permanently allocated to a data table view; this ratio shall not be user-configurable.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 fixes the display layout proportions to ensure consistent situational awareness presentation in the operations center.

generated_requirements.text: Within the data table region, the top one-quarter shall display sequential (order-dependent, non-time-bound) Events and the bottom three-quarters shall display Events ordered by temporal proximity to current exercise time.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 defines the segmentation logic of the data table to separate sequential from temporally relevant Events.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall color-code each displayed Event and Activity based on its variance from current exercise time, using color thresholds (e.g., on-time, at-risk, overdue) that conform to the DHS color schema standard.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 and BR-05 require color-coded schedule variance indicators traceable to the official DHS Visual Identity and Color Standards.

generated_requirements.text: In addition to color coding, the system shall display a supplementary status indicator (e.g., a text label or icon) alongside each Event and Activity to convey schedule variance status, ensuring that status information is not communicated by color alone.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act prohibits relying solely on color to convey information, requiring supplementary indicators for color-blind users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display two simultaneously visible, visually distinct clocks at all times: one showing current local (wall-clock) time and one showing current exercise time.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 requires dual-clock display to eliminate confusion in exercises where exercise time diverges from wall-clock time.

generated_requirements.text: The exercise time clock shall be sourced from or synchronized with the authoritative exercise clock service; the display shall show a visible warning indicator if synchronization with the exercise clock service is lost.
generated_requirements.rationale: Loss of exercise clock synchronization without notification would silently corrupt schedule variance calculations and mislead controllers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a Pause control that suspends the automatic 60-second data refresh cycle and a Resume control that restarts the 60-second cycle from the moment of resumption; the display shall clearly indicate when refresh is paused.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 permits manual pause of the refresh cycle to support briefing moments, and users must be clearly informed of the paused state to avoid acting on stale data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to filter the displayed Events and Activities by Venue, by Key Events designation, or by any combination of both dimensions simultaneously.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 mandates filtering by at least Venue and Key Events to reduce cognitive load during high-tempo exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an on-demand 'Full Exercise View' that clears all active filters and displays all Events and Activities in the exercise without restriction.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-13 requires an unfiltered view of all Events to be accessible at any time regardless of active filter state.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display only MSEL Summary data in both the graphical timeline nodes and the data table rows; full MSEL detail records shall not be rendered within EEMDS.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 restricts the data scope to MSEL Summary data to keep the display concise and within the defined system boundary.

generated_requirements.text: Each Event and Activity displayed in EEMDS shall include a direct deep-link that, when activated, opens the corresponding record in the originating source application (Sync Matrix 1.0 or EMT 1.0) without requiring the user to re-navigate within that application; EEMDS shall remain open and uninterrupted when the deep link is launched.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 prohibits editing within EEMDS and requires seamless navigation to the source application record to support data modification without context loss.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all changes to Event and Activity data — as reflected from source systems during refresh cycles — into a tamper-evident history report, capturing at minimum: the changed field, previous value, new value, timestamp of detection, and the refresh cycle in which the change was detected.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 and NIST SP 800-92 require a complete, integrity-protected audit trail of all data changes to support after-action review and federal log management standards.

generated_requirements.text: History report timestamps shall be derived from an NTP-synchronized trusted time source; the system shall record and display the time source reference used for each log entry.
generated_requirements.rationale: NIST SP 800-92 and audit log integrity requirements mandate that log timestamps be tied to a trusted, synchronized time source to ensure legal and regulatory sufficiency.

generated_requirements.text: The history report shall be retrievable by authorized users at any point during or after the exercise and shall be exportable in at least one structured format (e.g., CSV or PDF).
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 requires the history report to be available throughout and after the exercise to support AAR analysis and reporting.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate actual-versus-assumed time variance charts for Events and Activities, displaying the quantitative difference between the planned exercise time and the actual time at which each Event or Activity occurred.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD Future State and Scope sections identify actual-versus-assumed time variance charts as a required analytical output for post-exercise review.

generated_requirements.text: Authorized users shall be able to define, save, modify, and delete named time segments used to organize Events on the graphical timeline; time segment configurations shall be saveable per exercise and restorable for reuse.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 requires user-configurable time segments, and version-controlled per-exercise configurations support repeatable exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall save and restore the complete display configuration state — including active time segments, filter settings, and layout preferences — as a named, versioned configuration profile per exercise.
generated_requirements.rationale: Configuration baseline and change management patterns require saveable, restorable, version-controlled settings to support repeatable and auditable exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate every user at login using credentials validated against the externally managed authorized-user directory; access shall be denied to any user not present in the authorized-user list, and no guest or anonymous access mode shall exist.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 and DHS 4300A mandate positive authentication of all users with zero unauthorized access, with no anonymous access modes permitted.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC), granting each authenticated user only the permissions associated with their assigned role (e.g., Exercise Controller, Exercise Planner, AAR Analyst, IT Administrator); permission sets shall be defined and managed by the IT Administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: NIST SP 800-53 AC control family and the BRD stakeholder model require role-differentiated access to prevent unauthorized configuration changes or report access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically lock the user session and require re-authentication after a configurable period of inactivity (default: 15 minutes), consistent with DHS 4300A session management requirements.
generated_requirements.rationale: DHS 4300A and NIST SP 800-53 AC-11 require automatic session lock after inactivity to prevent unauthorized access on unattended workstations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all authentication events — including successful logins, failed login attempts, session locks, session unlocks, and logouts — with timestamps, user identifiers, and workstation identifiers, and shall retain these logs for a minimum of one year.
generated_requirements.rationale: NIST SP 800-53 AU control family and NIST SP 800-92 require comprehensive authentication event logging with defined retention periods for federal systems.

generated_requirements.text: When the system detects that Sync Matrix 1.0 or Exercise Management Tool 1.0 is unreachable or returns an error during a refresh cycle, it shall display a clearly visible data staleness warning indicating which source is unavailable and the timestamp of the last successful data retrieval from that source; it shall not silently display stale data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Graceful degradation behavior is a critical gap in the BRD; silently showing stale data during a live exercise can directly degrade exercise fidelity and decision-making.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a distinct Projection Display Mode with enlarged font sizes, high-contrast layout, and simplified visual elements optimized for readability at distances up to 100 feet on a 10×10 projection screen, toggled separately from the standard Operator Workstation Mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD identifies projection readability as a primary use case requiring a dedicated display mode distinct from the operator's close-range workstation view.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 in read-only mode using their existing documented data interfaces; no modifications to either source application are permitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-02 and BR-06 constrain EEMDS to read-only integration with source systems using existing interfaces to avoid scope creep and source system risk.

generated_requirements.text: If exercise scenario data (MSEL content) is designated as Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI) under the DHS CUI program, the system shall enforce CUI handling controls including access restrictions, on-screen CUI markings, and export controls on history reports.
generated_requirements.rationale: DHS CUI program requirements mandate that systems handling CUI-designated data enforce appropriate marking, handling, and dissemination controls.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete each 60-second data refresh cycle — including data retrieval from both source systems, processing, and full display update — within 10 seconds of the scheduled refresh trigger under normal operating conditions with a full exercise dataset.
generated_requirements.rationale: The 60-second refresh SLA requires that the refresh operation itself consume only a small fraction of the cycle to avoid perceptible display lag as required by OC-02.

generated_requirements.text: The graphical timeline and data table shall render without perceptible lag (target: under 2 seconds) when a user applies or clears a filter, switches display modes, or navigates between views.
generated_requirements.rationale: High-tempo exercise execution requires immediate visual feedback on user interactions to avoid controller distraction and decision delay.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be available 99% of the time during defined normal business hours (0800–1800 local time, Monday through Friday, and all scheduled exercise execution windows) over the first six months of operation; planned maintenance shall be scheduled outside these windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 3 and OC-01 establish a 99% availability SLA during operational hours as a measurable success criterion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from an unplanned crash or restart and restore the last saved display configuration and resume the data refresh cycle within 3 minutes without requiring manual reconfiguration by the operator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rapid, automatic recovery from failures is essential to maintain the 99% availability SLA and minimize disruption during live exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: All visual design elements — including backgrounds, text colors, highlight colors, status indicators, and chart colors — shall conform to the Department of Homeland Security Visual Identity and Color Standards document; no color choices outside this schema are permitted without formal DHS written approval.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 and RC-01 make DHS color schema compliance a non-negotiable regulatory constraint traceable to the official DHS standards document.

generated_requirements.text: The Projection Display Mode shall render all Event and Activity status indicators, text labels, and color codes legibly to 90% of viewers at a viewing distance of 100 feet on a 10×10 projection screen, as validated by user acceptance testing in a representative projection environment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 2 and TC-04 establish projection readability at 100 feet as a measurable success criterion for the display system.

generated_requirements.text: The Projection Display Mode shall render all Event and Activity detail data legibly to 90% of viewers at a viewing distance of 30 feet on a 10×10 projection screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 8 establishes a separate 30-foot readability criterion for detail-level data distinct from the 100-foot status indicator criterion.

generated_requirements.text: 90% of new users with no prior EEMDS experience shall be able to independently initiate an Event or Activity display within 90 minutes of first use, without formal training, as measured by structured usability testing.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 4 establishes a measurable learnability criterion to minimize the training burden on exercise controllers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act (29 U.S.C. § 794d) and WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards applicable to desktop software, ensuring that all status and informational content is accessible to users with visual impairments including color blindness.
generated_requirements.rationale: Federal agency software must meet Section 508 accessibility requirements; color-only status indicators are insufficient for color-blind users and must be supplemented.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a minimum password complexity policy and account lockout policy consistent with NIST SP 800-63B and DHS 4300A requirements for user authentication credentials.
generated_requirements.rationale: DHS 4300A and NIST SP 800-53 IA control family mandate minimum authentication strength standards for DHS operational systems.

generated_requirements.text: All data in transit between EEMDS and the source applications (Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0) shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher; unencrypted data transmission is not permitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: NIST SP 800-53 SC control family and DHS 4300A require encryption of data in transit for DHS internal operational systems handling potentially CUI-designated data.

generated_requirements.text: The history report and all audit logs shall be stored in a tamper-evident format; any unauthorized modification or deletion of log records shall be detectable, and write access to log storage shall be restricted to the system process only.
generated_requirements.rationale: NIST SP 800-53 AU-9 and NIST SP 800-92 require audit log integrity protection to ensure logs are legally and regulatorily sufficient for after-action and compliance review.

generated_requirements.text: Audit logs and history reports shall be retained for a minimum of three years or as required by applicable DHS records retention schedules, whichever is longer, and shall be protected against unauthorized deletion during the retention period.
generated_requirements.rationale: NIST SP 800-92 and federal records management requirements mandate defined retention periods and deletion protection for audit logs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with the FISMA Moderate baseline (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5) control families AC, AU, IA, SC, and SI, and shall produce documentation sufficient to support a DHS Risk Management Framework (RMF) Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) package and Authority to Operate (ATO).
generated_requirements.rationale: As a DHS operational tool, EEMDS cannot be deployed without a formal ATO; all security controls must be documented and assessable against the FISMA Moderate baseline.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable as a standalone Windows-based client application on a standard exercise operations center PC without requiring server-side rendering infrastructure or cloud connectivity for core display functionality.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-01 constrains deployment to a standard client PC environment, and the system must function without cloud or server dependencies to ensure operational resilience.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support deployment on Windows 10 and Windows 11 operating systems; the installation package shall complete without errors on a standard DHS-configured workstation without requiring elevated administrator privileges beyond initial installation.
generated_requirements.rationale: DHS operations center workstations run standard Windows configurations, and the system must be installable within those constraints to avoid IT deployment barriers.

generated_requirements.text: The system's integration layer shall be designed with documented, versioned interface adapters for Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0, such that a change to a source application's data interface requires modification only to the corresponding adapter without redesigning the core display application.
generated_requirements.rationale: Isolating source system integration into versioned adapters reduces the cost and risk of maintaining EEMDS when source application interfaces evolve.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall include an operator-accessible diagnostic log that records each refresh cycle's start time, completion time, data retrieval status per source system, record counts retrieved, and any errors encountered, without requiring access to system internals.
generated_requirements.rationale: Operator-accessible diagnostics enable IT administrators and system integrators to quickly identify and resolve integration failures during live exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: When multiple EEMDS instances are running simultaneously (e.g., operator workstation and projection display), all instances shall display identical exercise time and Event status data within the same 60-second refresh cycle, with no instance showing data from a different refresh cycle than others.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-instance display consistency is required to prevent contradictory situational awareness between the operator workstation and the room-wide projection display.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a visible, persistent staleness indicator showing the elapsed time since the last successful data refresh; this indicator shall update in real time and shall change to a warning state if the elapsed time exceeds 90 seconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Operators must always know the age of the data they are viewing; a 90-second threshold (1.5× the refresh cycle) provides an early warning of refresh failures before data becomes significantly stale.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a formal dependency on receipt of the DHS Visual Identity and Color Standards specification document prior to the start of UI design; UI design artifacts shall not be approved or baselined until this document has been received and reviewed by the development team.
generated_requirements.rationale: D-03 identifies the DHS color schema specification as a hard dependency; formalizing this as a requirement prevents premature UI design that would require costly rework.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide the IT Administrator with a user management interface to add, deactivate, and assign roles to authorized users; changes to user access shall take effect no later than the next login attempt by the affected user.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 and the IT Administrator stakeholder role require administrative tooling to manage the authorized-user list without modifying the external identity management infrastructure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a clear, non-dismissible warning banner if the DHS color schema specification has not been loaded or validated at application startup, preventing exercise execution until the schema is confirmed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ensuring the DHS color schema is active before display use prevents non-compliant color rendering during live exercises, which would violate RC-01.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict access to history reports and audit logs to users assigned the AAR Analyst or IT Administrator roles; Exercise Controllers and other roles shall not have access to export or delete log data.
generated_requirements.rationale: NIST SP 800-53 AU-9 and CUI handling requirements mandate that audit log access be restricted to authorized roles to protect log integrity and sensitive exercise scenario data.

sme_subject: DHS exercise management and operations center systems specialist
sme_advisory: EEMDS is a real-time situational awareness display tool operating in a high-stakes DHS exercise environment where display failures, data staleness, or access control gaps can directly degrade exercise fidelity and post-exercise accountability. The requirements engineer must pay close attention to the interplay between the 60-second refresh SLA and source system availability — degraded-mode behavior (e.g., what the display shows when Sync Matrix 1.0 or EMT 1.0 is unreachable) is a critical gap that the BRD does not fully address. Additionally, DHS IT security policy compliance (including FISMA, DHS 4300A, and applicable NIST SP 800-53 controls) will impose non-negotiable constraints on authentication, audit logging, and configuration management that must be explicitly captured as requirements rather than assumed.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall refresh the display every 60 seconds.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The application shall match the color of the schema set forth by Department of Homeland Security
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If projected the data must be readable. On a 10x10 projection screen 90% of viewers must be able to read Event / Activity data from a viewing distance of 30
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be available during normal business hours. As long as the user has access to the client PC the system will be available 99% of the time during the first six months of operation.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If projected the data must be understandable. On a 10x10 projection screen 90% of viewers must be able to determine that Events or Activities are occuring in current time from a viewing distance of 100
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall ensure that it can only be accessed by authorized users. The product will be able to distinguish between authorized and unauthorized users in all access attempts
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be intuitive and self-explanatory. : 90% of new users shall be able to start the display of Events or Activities within 90 minutes of using the product.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall respond fast to keep up-to-date data in the display.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall have a MDI form that allows for the viewing of the graph and the data table.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display Events in a vertical table by time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display the Events in a graph by time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display Events or Activities.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The display shall have two regions: left 2/3 of the display is graphical right 1/3 of the display is a data table
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The data displayed in both the nodes within the graph and the rows in the table are MSEL Summary data
ground_truth_requirements.label: NONE

ground_truth_requirements.text: The table side of the display shall be split into 2 regions: sequential and temporal.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The top 1/4 of the table will hold events that are to occur sequentially.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The bottom 3/4 of the table will hold events that occur according to its relevance to current time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall color code events according to their variance from current time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display data from the Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 applications
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall link Events back to either the Sync Matrix 1.0 or the Exercise Managment Tool 1.0 applications for modifications.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall allow modification of the display.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall offer a display of all the Events in the exercise.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall filter data by: Venues and Key Events.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall allow a user to define the time segments
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display the local and exercise time in separate clocks
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall offer the ability to pause and resume the refresh of data.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall provide charts for the Activity or Event actual versus assumed time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall provide a history report of changes made to the Activity or Event data
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require every user to authenticate with valid registered-realtor credentials (username and password) before granting access to any system function, denying access to any unregistered individual.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 mandates that only formally registered realtors may access the system, and every session must begin with authentication and authorization.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce MLS board-level access boundaries so that an authenticated realtor may only query, view, and modify listings from MLS boards to which their brokerage holds active membership; attempts to access out-of-scope boards shall be denied and logged.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS reciprocity rules prohibit agents from accessing listings outside their brokerage's authorized MLS board memberships.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow an authenticated realtor to search MLS property listings by specifying one or more criteria (e.g., location, price range, property type, number of bedrooms/bathrooms) and shall display matching results including at least one property image per listing.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS search with image display is a core field-productivity feature identified in Section 6.1 and the problem statement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return MLS property search results to the realtor within 30 seconds of query submission under normal operating conditions, 100% of the time.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD defines a hard 30-second performance ceiling for search results as a non-negotiable business success criterion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow an authenticated realtor to create a new MLS property listing from the mobile device by entering all RESO Data Dictionary mandatory fields; the system shall validate all field values against MLS-defined rules before submitting the listing to the MLS API.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 requires data validation before MLS submission, and RESO Data Dictionary compliance is required to prevent MLS board rejection of submissions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow an authenticated realtor to update an existing MLS property listing from the mobile device; all modified fields shall be validated against RESO Data Dictionary and MLS-defined rules before the update is submitted to the MLS API.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 includes MLS listing management (create and update) as an in-scope capability, with BR-02 requiring data integrity enforcement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record a timestamped, agent-attributed audit log entry for every MLS listing create and update operation, capturing the realtor's ID, the listing identifier, the fields modified, and the date/time of the operation.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS board rules and real estate industry practice require a traceable audit trail of all listing modifications for compliance and dispute resolution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display all MLS listing data in compliance with IDX display rules, including mandatory listing broker attribution, required field display, and prohibition of suppressing any fields mandated by the applicable MLS Data License Agreement.
generated_requirements.rationale: IDX/RESO compliance obligations require specific display rules for any system querying or presenting MLS data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow an authenticated realtor to generate a Comparative Market Analysis (CMA) report by entering property criteria; the system shall select comparable properties based on configurable filters including geographic radius, recency window, and property type, and shall display the selected comparables and their selection criteria to the realtor before finalizing the report.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 requires explicit criteria input for CMA generation, and domain expertise requires comp-selection transparency so agents and clients can validate methodology.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and deliver a completed CMA report to the realtor within 60 seconds of criteria entry submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD defines a hard 60-second performance ceiling for CMA report generation as a non-negotiable business success criterion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not generate a CMA report unless the realtor has explicitly provided all required property criteria; if mandatory criteria are missing, the system shall prompt the realtor to supply the missing values before proceeding.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 explicitly prohibits CMA generation without explicit criteria input from the user.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a map showing the geographic locations of properties matching a realtor's search criteria for a specified area, using data sourced exclusively from MLS listings accessible to the authenticated realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 and Section 6.1 require integrated property mapping limited to MLS data within the realtor's authorized scope.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide turn-by-turn driving directions from the realtor's current location to any property selected from the MLS search results, using an integrated third-party mapping service, and shall only offer directions for properties present in the MLS dataset accessible to the authenticated realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 and Section 6.1 require in-app driving directions to eliminate the need for separate navigation tools.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow an authenticated realtor to download, view, and manage their client contacts and scheduled appointments on the mobile device.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 identifies contact and appointment management as a core in-scope capability to address field data fragmentation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically synchronize contact and appointment data with the office system once every hour without requiring any action from the realtor, ensuring that on-device data is never more than 60 minutes stale.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 mandates automatic hourly synchronization as a fixed business constraint for data freshness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform delta synchronization during each hourly sync cycle, transferring only records that have changed since the previous successful synchronization rather than full dataset payloads, to accommodate the limited RAM, storage, and network throughput of Windows CE and Palm OS devices.
generated_requirements.rationale: Full-payload sync on legacy Palm/WinCE devices with constrained resources will frequently time out or exhaust storage; delta sync is required for reliability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the realtor immediately upon detecting a new appointment or a client response to an appointment request during automatic synchronization, without requiring the realtor to manually check for updates.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 requires automated appointment notifications to eliminate scheduling lag identified in the problem statement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall initiate telephone calls to sellers or buyers to schedule appointments on behalf of the realtor, using only contact phone numbers associated with that realtor's client records, and shall attribute all such communications to the realtor's account in the audit log.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 and Section 6.1 require automated appointment-call initiation traceable to the realtor's account.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce TCPA compliance for all system-initiated calls to buyers and sellers, including: verifying prior express written consent is on record before placing a call, checking the applicable do-not-call registry, and restricting call placement to between 8:00 AM and 9:00 PM in the recipient's local time zone.
generated_requirements.rationale: TCPA requires prior express written consent and time-of-day restrictions for automated calls to consumers; non-compliance exposes the organization to significant legal liability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to save property search results to the device for later review; saved results shall remain accessible in offline mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 requires that saved search results be persistable and accessible offline.

generated_requirements.text: When internet connectivity is unavailable, the system shall operate in offline mode, providing read-only access to previously downloaded search results, CMA reports, contacts, and appointments; write operations that cannot be completed offline shall be queued locally and submitted automatically upon reconnection.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 mandates offline read access and write-operation queuing to ensure field productivity regardless of connectivity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement a defined conflict resolution policy for offline write queues: when an offline-queued record (contact or appointment) conflicts with a server-side change made during the offline period, the system shall present the conflict to the realtor with both versions and require explicit realtor selection of the version to retain before committing the record.
generated_requirements.rationale: Offline write-queue conflicts with server-side state changes are a critical edge case that must have a defined resolution strategy to prevent silent data loss.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement a tiered degradation model with three explicitly defined modes: (1) Full Online Mode — all features available; (2) Partial Connectivity Mode — synchronization and MLS queries available but with extended timeouts; (3) Full Offline Mode — read-only access to cached data, write operations queued. The system shall detect the current connectivity tier automatically and communicate the active mode to the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: A defined graceful degradation hierarchy ensures predictable behavior across connectivity states and sets clear expectations for the realtor.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall save and restore all user preferences — including search filters, display settings, and saved results — across sessions and system failures, ensuring 100% of saved preferences are restored to their last known state following any failure or connectivity interruption.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 and the business success criteria require complete preference persistence and restoration as a data integrity guarantee.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a maximum on-device retention period of 7 days for cached MLS listing data, CMA reports, and contact records; data exceeding this retention period shall be automatically purged or flagged as stale, consistent with MLS Data License Agreement terms prohibiting indefinite local storage.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS data license terms typically prohibit indefinite local storage of listing data; a defined purge policy ensures compliance and prevents stale data from misleading agents.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall cache MLS property images locally on the device, enforcing a maximum image resolution of 800×600 pixels and a total image cache size limit appropriate to the target device storage constraints; when device storage is full, the system shall evict the least-recently-used images first and notify the realtor if image loading fails due to storage exhaustion.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS property images are large; resolution and cache-size limits are required to prevent storage exhaustion on memory-constrained Windows CE and Palm OS devices.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall handle an expired authentication session token gracefully when the device cannot reach the authentication server: the system shall permit continued read-only access to locally cached data for up to 4 hours after token expiry, after which all access shall be suspended until re-authentication is completed upon connectivity restoration.
generated_requirements.rationale: Agents frequently lose signal mid-session; a defined token-expiry grace period balances security with field usability in low-connectivity environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be available 24 hours per day, 365 days per year, achieving a minimum availability rate of 99.9% (no more than 1 failed access attempt per 1,000 attempts).
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD defines 99.9% availability as a non-negotiable business success criterion to maximize agent productivity at all hours.

generated_requirements.text: The system backend shall support a minimum of 1,000 simultaneous authenticated user sessions at launch, and the architecture shall be designed to scale to 5,000 simultaneous users within 12 months without requiring a system redesign.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD mandates scalability from 1,000 to 5,000 concurrent users within 12 months to accommodate the organization's fivefold growth projection.

generated_requirements.text: The system architecture shall be designed to handle the proportional increase in MLS query traffic, synchronization payload volume, and on-device cached data footprint resulting from a fivefold increase in registered users, without degradation of the 30-second search and 60-second CMA performance ceilings.
generated_requirements.rationale: A fivefold user increase multiplies MLS query traffic and sync payload sizes in ways that a simple user-count metric does not capture; explicit data-volume scalability must be addressed.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall run natively on Windows CE and Palm OS operating systems; no other mobile platforms are in scope for this release.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 7.1 constrains the target platforms exclusively to Windows CE and Palm OS for this release.

generated_requirements.text: The application user interface shall conform to the native UI standards and conventions of the target platform (Windows CE or Palm OS), including use of standard platform menus, navigation buttons, and interaction patterns.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 7.1 requires UI conformance to native platform standards to ensure intuitive operation on target devices.

generated_requirements.text: At least 90% of untrained realtors shall be able to successfully install the application on their device without printed instructions or IT assistance, as measured by unassisted installation testing with a representative sample of the target user population.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD defines a 90% unassisted-installation success rate as a measurable business success criterion to eliminate onboarding overhead.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall be usable by realtors with no formal training on the product; all UI labels, terminology, and workflows shall use language and concepts natural to the real estate profession, and all primary tasks shall be completable without reference to a user manual.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 7.2 and the business objectives require zero-training usability to eliminate onboarding overhead and enable immediate field productivity.

generated_requirements.text: All sensitive data stored on the device — including cached MLS listings, client contact records, CMA reports, user credentials, and authentication tokens — shall be encrypted at rest using a minimum of AES-128 encryption.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cached PII and proprietary MLS data on mobile devices require encryption at rest to comply with regulatory best practice and applicable state data protection laws.

generated_requirements.text: All network communications between the mobile application and backend services (authentication server, synchronization server, MLS API, mapping service) shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher; the application shall bundle a compliant TLS library where the native Windows CE or Palm OS platform does not provide one, and shall enforce certificate validation with a minimum cipher suite of AES-128-GCM with SHA-256.
generated_requirements.rationale: Windows CE and Palm OS lack native TLS 1.2/1.3 support; bundling a compliant library and enforcing certificate validation is required to secure data in transit on these legacy platforms.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce authorization checks on every access attempt to every system function, ensuring that no system capability is accessible without a valid, current authorization context; zero unauthorized access incidents are permitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD defines zero unauthorized access incidents as a non-negotiable security success criterion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ensure that MLS listing data displayed on the mobile device is scoped exclusively to the authenticated realtor's authorized access boundaries and is not exposed beyond that scope, in compliance with state real estate licensing laws governing listing data advertisement.
generated_requirements.rationale: State real estate licensing laws restrict which agents may display certain listing data; unauthorized exposure creates legal liability for the brokerage.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from any failure or connectivity interruption and restore 100% of saved user preferences to their last known state upon restart, with no manual intervention required from the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 and the business success criteria require complete, automatic preference restoration following any failure event.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect loss of internet connectivity within 30 seconds and automatically transition to offline mode, displaying a clear notification to the realtor indicating that offline mode is active and specifying which features are unavailable.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely and transparent connectivity-state detection is required to prevent realtors from unknowingly operating on stale data or attempting unavailable operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically detect restoration of internet connectivity and transition from offline mode to the appropriate online mode, resuming automatic synchronization and flushing any queued offline write operations without requiring realtor intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic reconnection handling ensures data consistency is restored without burdening the realtor with manual steps after connectivity returns.

generated_requirements.text: The application codebase shall be structured to allow independent maintenance and updates of the Windows CE and Palm OS platform-specific modules without requiring changes to shared business logic components.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supporting two legacy platforms requires a modular architecture to minimize the cost and risk of platform-specific bug fixes and updates.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all synchronization cycle outcomes — including start time, end time, number of records transferred, and any errors encountered — to a persistent log accessible to the IT/system administrator for monitoring and troubleshooting.
generated_requirements.rationale: Synchronization failure visibility is critical for IT to diagnose and resolve data-freshness issues before they impact agent productivity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall load and display MLS property images using a progressive loading strategy: a low-resolution placeholder shall be displayed immediately while the full-resolution image loads, and if the full-resolution image is already cached locally it shall be displayed without a network request.
generated_requirements.rationale: Progressive image loading is required to maintain perceived performance on slow cellular connections typical of Windows CE and Palm OS field devices.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate that all MLS listing create and update payloads include all RESO Data Dictionary mandatory fields for the applicable property type before submission; submissions missing mandatory fields shall be rejected by the application with a specific error message identifying each missing field.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS boards reject submissions that omit required RESO Data Dictionary fields; client-side validation prevents failed submissions and protects data integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall capture and store a record of prior express written consent from each buyer or seller contact before the system is permitted to initiate any automated call to that contact for appointment scheduling purposes.
generated_requirements.rationale: TCPA requires documented prior express written consent before placing automated calls to consumers; absence of this record exposes the organization to statutory penalties.

generated_requirements.text: The self-service installation package shall include all required runtime libraries and dependencies for the target platform (Windows CE or Palm OS), such that the realtor does not need to separately install any prerequisite software, including the bundled TLS library.
generated_requirements.rationale: Self-service installation success requires a self-contained package; missing dependencies are a primary cause of installation failure for non-technical users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display the current synchronization status to the realtor at all times, including the timestamp of the last successful synchronization, so the realtor can assess the freshness of on-device data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Realtors need to know how current their data is to make informed decisions about whether to rely on cached information or wait for a sync.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a maximum on-device total storage budget for all cached application data (listings, images, CMA reports, contacts, appointments) appropriate to the minimum storage capacity of supported Windows CE and Palm OS devices, and shall alert the realtor when available cache storage falls below 10% of the allocated budget.
generated_requirements.rationale: Windows CE and Palm OS devices have severely constrained storage; a defined storage budget and low-storage alert prevents application failure due to storage exhaustion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with all applicable MLS Data License Agreement terms governing the use, display, and local storage of MLS data, and shall provide the brokerage's IT/compliance team with a configuration mechanism to update display attribution strings and data-use policy parameters without requiring an application code change.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS Data License Agreement terms can change; a configurable compliance layer allows the organization to respond to policy changes without a full application release cycle.

domain: real estate technology / mobile field productivity
sme_subject: MLS integration architect and mobile real estate platform specialist
sme_advisory: This system sits at the intersection of MLS data governance, mobile field productivity, and real-time synchronization — three domains each carrying their own non-obvious constraints. The requirements engineer should pay particular attention to MLS RESO/IDX compliance obligations, the severe limitations of Windows CE and Palm OS platforms (memory, storage, network stack maturity), and the behavioral edge cases that arise when offline-queued write operations conflict with server-side state changes during reconnection. Scalability requirements must be expressed in terms of both concurrent session load and data volume growth, since a fivefold user increase will also multiply MLS query traffic, cached data footprints, and synchronization payload sizes in ways that a simple user-count metric will not capture.
